title: plantago maritima subsp. borealis
intro: Plantago maritima subsp. borealis, also known as sea plantain, is a subspecies of the Plantago maritima plant, a perennial herb native to the coastal areas of the Northern Hemisphere. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds, skin irritations, and infections. The health benefits of sea plantain include reducing inflammation and improving wound healing, as well as providing antioxidant and antimicrobial effects. Sea plantain can be prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or salve, and is often used in traditional medicine to treat a range of health issues.
